title: Create And Import Endpoints
content_html:
Docs Viewer Create And Import Endpoints

POST /docs/create

Expected data:

{
  "scope": "studio",
  "title": "New Doc",
  "parent_id": "d-20260424-000000-50b63f"
}

Actions:

  • validates scope against configured docs scopes
  • defaults blank titles to New Doc
  • allocates an immutable doc_id and matching filename independently of the title
  • validates parent_id inside the same scope when supplied
  • writes a new Markdown source file under the configured scope root
  • writes matching second-resolution added_date and last_updated values
  • omits viewable for local-scope docs such as Studio, which makes them viewable by default
  • writes viewable: false for public-scope docs such as Analysis, Library, and Moments
  • rebuilds targeted generated docs payloads and targeted docs search for the new doc
  • writes watcher-suppression markers for the new source file

Returned data includes the created doc identity, source path, viewer URL, mutation flags, rebuild diagnostics, summary text, and dry_run.

Copy Subtree Endpoints

POST /docs/copy-subtree-preview

Expected data:

{
  "scope": "studio",
  "source_doc_id": "d-20260424-000000-50b63f",
  "target_scope": "notes"
}

The preview is write-free. It validates two different configured scopes, excludes public target scopes, requires a readable source root and a non-public target root that can accept managed canonical writes, expands the complete source subtree in deterministic tree order, captures one copy timestamp, and allocates a new immutable identity and matching target filename for every copied document. Public scopes remain valid sources but are never offered or accepted as copy targets.

The response names the source root, target scope, target-root placement, document and descendant counts, and an apply_plan. The apply plan is a bounded server-issued receipt containing planned identities plus source/configuration digests. The browser returns it unchanged; it is not a browser identity-allocation or source-path surface.

POST /docs/copy-subtree-apply

Expected data:

{
  "scope": "studio",
  "confirm": true,
  "apply_plan": {
    "schema_version": "docs_copy_subtree_apply_plan_v1",
    "source_scope": "studio",
    "source_doc_id": "d-20260424-000000-50b63f",
    "target_scope": "notes",
    "copy_timestamp": "2026-07-16 21:00:00",
    "source_config_sha256": "...",
    "target_config_sha256": "...",
    "documents": [
      {
        "source_doc_id": "d-20260424-000000-50b63f",
        "source_sha256": "...",
        "target_doc_id": "d-20260716-210000-a1b2c3"
      }
    ]
  }
}

Apply requires confirm: true and does not accept dry_run. Before the first write it restores the previewed plan and revalidates current scope configuration, source content and subtree order, the copy timestamp, every planned identity and parent, every target filename, and the absence of target collisions. A stale or changed preview is rejected and no existing document is overwritten.

The copied root is placed at the target scope root. Descendant parents and copied-set Docs Viewer links are remapped to new target identities. Other front matter and body content are preserved except for identity, timestamps, parent, and target-default viewability. External links, semantic references, media and download references, and interactive assets retain their original targets; no asset bytes are copied.

One confirmed apply writes only new target canonical files, adds watcher suppression for those paths, and runs one target generated-docs and targeted search rebuild. It records one bounded docs-copy-subtree activity event after a successful rebuild. The response includes source and target scopes, the new root id, all created ids, document count, copy timestamp, viewer-link rewrite count, rebuild diagnostics, summary text, and the target manage-viewer URL.

A rebuild failure is returned as an apply failure and no success activity is recorded. Canonical target files already written before generation are not rolled back; the ordinary management write/rebuild boundary reports that partial follow-through rather than overwriting or silently retrying it.

GET /docs/import-source-files

Query parameters: none.

Returned data:

{
  "ok": true,
  "files": [
    {
      "filename": "example.md",
      "path": "$DOTLINEFORM_PROJECTS_BASE_DIR/data-sharing/import-staging/example.md",
      "source_format": "markdown",
      "size_bytes": 0,
      "modified_utc": "..."
    }
  ]
}

Used for:

  • rendering staged-file choices in the Docs Viewer import modal
  • showing file metadata before an import writes source docs

Supported staged source formats:

  • html: .html, .htm
  • docx: .docx
  • markdown: .md, .markdown
  • markdown_package: direct child package folders containing exactly one Markdown file
  • text: .txt
  • data_sharing_documents: trusted supported Data Sharing .json or .jsonl document collections

Standalone raster, SVG, and downloadable files are excluded. They belong to the active source editor's Add image… and Add file… actions.

Trusted returned document packages are classified directly from their staged header and internal export metadata. Docs Review package identity is not attached to the import listing and does not preselect a staged source.

POST /docs/import-source

Expected data:

{
  "scope": "studio",
  "staged_filename": "example.md",
  "include_prompt_meta": false,
  "confirm_interactive_html_overwrite": false,
  "preview_only": false
}

Actions:

  • validates scope and resolves staged_filename as a direct child of the W0-configured shared import drop-zone
  • parses staged HTML, Markdown, Markdown packages, text, and trusted document collections
  • converts import output to Markdown source
  • validates generated Markdown with the shared Docs Viewer Markdown renderer
  • uses the staged filename/title as source context and always allocates a fresh local document identity
  • does not load target-scope documents or infer document replacement from filenames or titles
  • atomically creates a new source document and refuses a last-moment target-path conflict
  • uses confirm_interactive_html_overwrite: true only to confirm replacement of a colliding role-marked interactive HTML companion
  • sets last_updated when the new document is created
  • materializes inline raster or sanitized SVG media, package media, and interactive HTML assets only during write operations
  • supports preview_only: true for non-writing preview responses
  • rebuilds targeted generated docs payloads and targeted docs search after successful writes
  • logs import-source activity when a create actually writes

Returned data can include preview Markdown, allocated document identity, operation type, media plans, interactive HTML plans, written media records, created source path, rebuild diagnostics, summary text, and dry_run.

Reviewed-Package Collection Import

Schema-aware Data Sharing JSON/JSONL collection planning and synchronous apply reuse POST /docs/import-source. The user selects the trusted server-listed staged package from the normal Docs Viewer Import Action.

The endpoints now use the shared $DOTLINEFORM_PROJECTS_BASE_DIR/data-sharing/import-staging/ drop-zone. Endpoint services resolve it through configured_workspace_paths(repo_root).import_staging, return marker-rooted paths, and report import unavailable through the W0 workspace capability contract when the root cannot be used. They do not fall back to repo-local staging.

The service resolves a safe immutable staged-file identity through Data Sharing metadata, detects supported package headers before the generic JSON/JSONL file fallback, and normalizes document records before shared validation, media planning, source writes, and rebuild work. It never imports from the derived Docs Review source/*.md projection.

Collection planning uses the ordinary request identity with preview_only: true:

{
  "scope": "library",
  "staged_filename": "returned-documents.jsonl",
  "preview_only": true
}

The body-free response covers every package record and includes package identity, server-planned create or overwrite actions, collisions, content intent, parent resolution, new-parent dependencies, media summaries, blockers, warnings, and confirmation readiness. Any invalid record or hierarchy error blocks the package. Planning never writes source or media and never invokes generation.

Confirmed apply uses preview_only: false, explicit confirmation, and the package identity, planned local identities, and planned package actions returned by the server:

{
  "scope": "library",
  "staged_filename": "returned-documents.jsonl",
  "preview_only": false,
  "confirm": true,
  "export_id": "ds_...",
  "source_sha256": "...",
  "planned_identities": [
    {
      "record_index": 0,
      "source_doc_id": "package-doc",
      "doc_id": "d-20260715-120000-a1b2c3",
      "added_date": "2026-07-15 12:00:00"
    }
  ],
  "planned_actions": [
    {
      "record_index": 0,
      "action": "overwrite",
      "doc_id": "d-20260101-120000-b2c3d4",
      "target_doc_id": "d-20260101-120000-b2c3d4"
    }
  ]
}

planned_actions contains only server-issued create or overwrite actions for the complete valid package. Collection apply rejects record decisions, browser-authored target paths, generated source, and fields outside the request allowlist. planned_identities and planned_actions are confirmed-plan receipts, not browser allocation or selection surfaces.

Apply rereads the staged package and recomputes the current plan. Changed package identity, server-planned actions, hierarchy validity, or blocker state returns preview_only: true, reconfirmation_required: true, and a refreshed plan without writes. If the confirmed plan remains valid, records are written synchronously in package order. A source-write failure preserves earlier writes, stops later writes, and marks remaining records not attempted; asset failures warn and continue. Docs and search generation runs once after mutation and is reported separately from source outcomes.

The final response includes outcome, source_mutation, generation, ordered records, grouped and counted created/overwritten/failed/not-attempted records, warnings, manual-copy instructions, and a marker-rooted report_path. Confirmed applies write the report under import-staging/results/; report-write failure adds a warning without changing the import result.
